schema.description: Array of Optimus subscription offer screen configurations, fetched from remote settings key 'optimus_screen_content'. Each entry defines component references for one screen variant. The app matches entries by content_id against the requested screen name, falling back to the entry with content_id 'default' if no match is found. Changing the order has no effect; only content_id matching matters.

radio_buttons.description: Configuration for the subscription plan radio buttons section including layout style, individual button items, double-tap purchase behavior, and indicator alignment. Each button item references a SubscriptionButtonApiModel by id from the 'subscription_button' settings list. If null, no radio buttons or CTA button are displayed.

indicator_alignment.description: Controls the position of the radio selection indicator relative to button content, parsed via parseIndicatorAlignment(). Accepted values: 'right' (indicator on the right side), 'none' (no indicator shown), or any other value/null (defaults to left). Changing this repositions or hides the selection indicator circle on all radio buttons.

buttons.description: Ordered list of radio button item configurations. Each item references a subscription button component by id and defines selection state, colors, border, badge, and layout. The array order determines the display order of subscription plan options on screen.

badge.description: Configuration for a small badge label displayed on the radio button (e.g. 'BEST VALUE', 'SAVE 50%'). Badge text supports %%package_id%% tokens that are replaced at runtime with pricing from that package. If null, no badge is shown on this radio button.

color.description: Text color of the badge label when the radio button is in the selected state, mapped to badgeTextConfig.color. Accepted values: a CascadeColorPackage path or hex color string. Changing this alters the badge text color for the selected radio button.

deselected_text_color.description: Text color of the badge label when the radio button is in the deselected state. Accepted values: a CascadeColorPackage path or hex color string. Changing this alters the badge text color for non-selected radio buttons.

deselected_bg_color.description: Background color of the badge when the radio button is in the deselected state. Accepted values: a CascadeColorPackage path or hex color string. Changing this alters the badge background fill for non-selected radio buttons.

variant.description: Visual variant identifier for the badge component rendering style. Accepted values: any string recognized by the badge view renderer. Changing this may alter the badge's shape, size, or visual treatment.

hide_on_deselected_state.description: Controls whether the badge is hidden when the radio button is deselected. Defaults to false when null (code uses orFalse()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to true makes the badge disappear when the user selects a different radio button.

text.description: Text displayed inside the badge label. Supports %%<package_id>%% tokens: the text between %% delimiters is extracted as a package identifier and the entire token is replaced with formatted price information via PaymentUseCase.replaceSpecialCharacter(). Accepted values: any string, optionally containing %%...%% placeholders. Changing this updates the badge label content on the radio button.

selected_bg_color.description: Background color applied to this radio button in the selected state. Accepted values: a CascadeColorPackage design-token path string or hex color. Changing this alters the background fill color of the radio button when the user selects it.

disabled_text_color.description: Text color applied to this radio button in the deselected state. Accepted values: a CascadeColorPackage design-token path string or hex color. Changing this alters the text color of the radio button's labels when it is not selected.

selected_border_color.description: Border color applied to this radio button when it is selected. Accepted values: a color path string or hex color. Changing this alters the visible border outline around the radio button in its selected state.

is_selected.description: Determines the initial selection state of this radio button when the screen first loads. Accepted values: true, false. The selected button's background/border/text switch to their selected-state colors, and its package is used for the CTA button label and the default purchase flow target.

is_enlarged_button_component.description: Controls whether this radio button uses an enlarged/expanded layout variant for visual emphasis. Accepted values: true, false, null. Setting to true renders the radio button with a larger component size.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionButtonApiModel from 'subscription_button' settings. Its package_info_id is then used to resolve subscription package pricing via GetProcessedPackagesUseCase. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that list. Changing this swaps the CTA button colors/style, main button colors, secondary CTA styling, and the package whose price data populates this radio button's text.

disabled_background_color.description: Background color applied to this radio button in the deselected state. Accepted values: a CascadeColorPackage design-token path string or hex color. Changing this alters the background fill of the radio button when it is not selected.

border_color.description: Border color applied to this radio button in the deselected state (mapped from disabledBorderColor). Accepted values: a color path string or hex color. Changing this alters the border outline of the radio button when it is not selected.

gradient_type.description: Gradient type applied to the badge background on this radio button, mapped to RadioBoxBadge.gradientType. Accepted values: any string recognized by the badge gradient renderer. Changing this alters the gradient fill style of the badge background.

purchase_on_select.description: Controls whether double-tapping a radio button immediately triggers the subscription purchase flow. Mapped to RadioButtonEntity.purchaseOnDoubleTap. Accepted values: true, false. When true and the user double-taps a selected radio button, the app opens the subscription purchase activity for that package without requiring a separate CTA button tap.

type.description: Determines which radio button component layout is inflated via parseRadioStyle(). Accepted values: the TIERS_STYLE constant (renders a tiers-style layout), or any other value/null (renders the default radio button layout). Changing this switches between the tiers-style and default-style radio button component on the offer screen.

trial_reminder_toggle.description: Reference to a trial reminder opt-in toggle component. Looked up from 'offer_promotion_toggle'. Shown when a free-trial-eligible radio button exists or when the switch_package_toggle has free trial data. The toggle is muted (disabled) when the selected radio button's promotion type is not FreeTrial.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the trial reminder toggle is fetched and rendered. Defaults to false when null (code uses orFalse()), unlike most other components which default to true. Accepted values: true, false. Must be explicitly set to true to show the trial reminder toggle on the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionToggleApiModel from 'offer_promotion_toggle'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the trial reminder toggle's enabled/disabled state data (title, subtitle, description, packageId) and view styling (text color, accent color).

toggle_placeholder_text.description: Reference to a placeholder text component displayed near the toggle area. Looked up from 'offer_toggle_placeholder_text'. This is a required (non-nullable) field in the data model. The looked-up SubscriptionTitleApiModel's title field is mapped to a TextConfig with text and color via titleComponentMapper.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the toggle placeholder text is fetched and rendered. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to false hides the toggle placeholder text from the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionTitleApiModel from 'offer_toggle_placeholder_text'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the toggle placeholder text content and text color displayed near the toggle component.

feature_highlights.description: Reference to a feature highlights component displaying a vertical list of items with icons, titles, and subtitles. Looked up from 'offer_screen_feature_highlights'. If null or disabled, no feature highlights are rendered. Only displayed on the full-screen Optimus offer screen.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the feature highlights component is fetched and rendered. Defaults to false when null (code uses orFalse()). Accepted values: true, false. Must be explicitly set to true to show feature highlights on the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a FeatureHighlightsApiModel from 'offer_screen_feature_highlights'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the list of feature highlight items (each with icon URL, title text/color, and subtitle text/color) displayed on the offer screen.

content_id.description: Unique identifier for this screen configuration, matched against the requested screen name in OptimusScreenRepoImpl.getSingleScreenData(). Accepted values: any string; the code explicitly checks for 'default' as a fallback when no screen name matches. Changing this value determines which touchpoint or variant selects this screen configuration; if it no longer matches any requested name, this entry becomes unreachable (unless it is 'default').

footer.description: Reference to a footer text component displayed below the radio buttons area. Looked up from 'offer_screen_footers'. The footer supports an optional click action that opens a deep link via HookUtils. If null, disabled, or the looked-up model's text is empty, the footer is hidden (visibility GONE).

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the footer component is fetched and rendered. Defaults to false when null (code uses orFalse()). Accepted values: true, false. Must be explicitly set to true to show the footer on the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a FooterApiModel from 'offer_screen_footers'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the footer text, text color, and click action deep link displayed below the radio buttons.

banner.description: Reference to a banner media component displayed at the top of the screen. Looked up from 'offer_screen_banners'. The banner supports multiple media types and clickable items that open deep links. If null or disabled, no banner is rendered.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the banner component is fetched and rendered. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to false removes the banner media (image/video/gif/lottie/reviews) from the top of the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionBannerApiModel from 'offer_screen_banners'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the banner's media type, list of banner value URLs/actions/placeholders.

description.description: Reference to a description text component displayed below the subtitle. Looked up from 'offer_screen_descriptions'. Rendered with Typography.T8 and FontWeights.REGULAR. If null, disabled, or the looked-up model's text is empty, the description TextView is hidden (visibility GONE).

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the description component is fetched and rendered. Defaults to false when null (code uses orFalse()). Accepted values: true, false. Must be explicitly set to true to show the description text on the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionTitleApiModel from 'offer_screen_descriptions'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the description text content and text color displayed below the subtitle.

title.description: Reference to a title component displayed below the banner and tab switcher. Looked up from 'offer_screen_titles'. In the full-screen fragment, if this field is null or disabled, the subtitle value is promoted to the title position (and subtitle is then hidden). In the half-screen fragment, the title behaves independently. If null/disabled and no fallback applies, the title TextView is hidden (visibility GONE).

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the title component is fetched and displayed. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to false hides the title TextView (visibility GONE) on the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionTitleApiModel from 'offer_screen_titles'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the title text string and text color rendered with Typography.T6 and FontWeights.SEMI_BOLD.

close_button.description: Reference to a close button component shown in the top corner of the screen. Looked up from 'subscription_screen_close_button'. If null (no config returned), a default close button appears at the top-start position. If the looked-up model has show=true, a custom close button is rendered with configurable icon, alignment, and margins.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the custom close button config is fetched. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. When false, the close button mapper receives null and the fragment falls back to showing a default close button at top-start with the standard icon.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a CloseButtonModel from 'subscription_screen_close_button'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the close button visibility, icon URL, alignment (left/right), top margin, and side margin.

subtitle.description: Reference to a subtitle component displayed below the title. Looked up from 'offer_screen_subtitles'. In the full-screen fragment, if the title field is null/disabled, this subtitle is promoted to the title position and the subtitle area is hidden. In the half-screen fragment, the subtitle behaves independently. If null, disabled, or the looked-up model's subtitle text is null, the subtitle TextView is hidden (visibility GONE).

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the subtitle component is fetched and displayed. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to false hides the subtitle TextView (visibility GONE) on the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionTitleApiModel (its subtitle field) from 'offer_screen_subtitles'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the subtitle text string and text color rendered with Typography.T5 and FontWeights.MEDIUM.

banner_placeholder.description: Reference to a banner placeholder component that displays large overlay text and an optional badge image over the banner area. Looked up from 'offer_banner_placeholder_text' remote settings. If null or disabled, no placeholder text or badge appears over the banner.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the banner placeholder component is fetched and rendered. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to false prevents the banner placeholder text, badge image, and gradient shadow from appearing on the screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionBannerPlaceholderApiModel from the 'offer_banner_placeholder_text' remote settings list. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that list. Changing this swaps the placeholder text, text color, badge image URL, and shadow-enabled flag displayed over the banner area.

secondary_button.description: Reference to a secondary action button displayed as underlined text below the subtitle. Looked up from 'offer_screen_secondary_button'. On click, it either opens a deep link via HookUtils (if action is non-empty) or opens a tiers half-screen fragment. If null or disabled, the secondary button is hidden (visibility GONE).

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the secondary button is fetched and displayed. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to false hides the underlined secondary button from the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionSecondaryButtonApiModel from 'offer_screen_secondary_button'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the underlined button text, text color (resolved via CascadeColorPackage), click action deep link, and the touchpoint string passed to the tiers half-screen.

logo.description: Reference to a logo image component displayed between the banner and tab switcher on the full-screen Optimus offer screen. Looked up from 'offer_screen_logos'. Supports configurable width and height dimensions. If null, disabled, or the looked-up model's URL is empty, the logo is hidden (visibility GONE). Not rendered in the half-screen fragment.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the logo component is fetched and rendered. Defaults to false when null (code uses orFalse()). Accepted values: true, false. Must be explicitly set to true to show the logo image on the full-screen offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a LogoApiModel from 'offer_screen_logos'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the logo image URL, width, and height displayed above the tab switcher on the full-screen offer screen.

switch_package_toggle.description: Reference to a free-trial/package-switching toggle component. Looked up from 'offer_promotion_toggle'. When the toggle's disabled_state.data.package_id matches a radio button's package_info_id, the toggle allows switching between regular and free-trial packages for that button. The toggle is shown only when a matching free-trial-eligible radio button exists.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the switch package toggle is fetched and rendered. Defaults to true when null (code uses orTrue()). Accepted values: true, false. Setting to false prevents the free-trial/package-switching toggle from appearing on the offer screen.

id.description: Identifier used to look up a SubscriptionToggleApiModel from 'offer_promotion_toggle'. Accepted values: any string matching an id in that settings list. Changing this swaps the toggle's enabled/disabled state configuration including title, subtitle, description, package_id, text color, and accent color.

$stable.description: Kotlin Compose compiler-generated stability marker. Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

only_pro_content_id.description: When a Plus subscriber or a user who used only Pro editor tools opens the offer screen and tabs would normally be shown, this ID overrides the tabbed layout and loads a single-screen offer from 'optimus_screen_content' (or 'optimus_half_screen_content') instead. Accepted values: any string matching a content_id entry in the screen-content settings. Changing it alters which single-offer screen Pro-only and Plus users see; removing it causes those users to see the normal tabbed layout.

screen_name.description: Identifies this configuration entry so the app can select it by matching against the contentVariant parameter (sourced from the touchpoint that opened the offer screen). Accepted values: any string; the literal 'default' acts as a fallback when no other entry matches. Changing it determines which touchpoints resolve to this configuration, and it is also sent as the 'offer_variant' analytics parameter.

content_id.description: When non-null and non-empty, the screen displays a single offer layout (no tabs) by loading content from 'optimus_screen_content' (or 'optimus_half_screen_content') whose content_id matches this value. Accepted values: any string that corresponds to a content_id in the screen-content settings. If set, the tabs array is ignored and a single-screen offer is shown; if null or empty, the app falls back to the tab-based layout defined by the 'tabs' array.

tabs.description: Array of tab configurations that populate the CycleSwitcherView tab bar at the top of the offer screen. If the array contains more than one entry, a tabbed layout is rendered and users can switch between offers; if it contains exactly one entry, that entry's content is loaded as a single-screen layout without a tab bar. Changing the number or order of entries directly controls whether tabs appear and which subscription offers are accessible.

badge.description: Displayed as a small badge label overlaid on the tab in the CycleSwitcherView (e.g. 'Best Value'). Accepted values: any string. Changing it updates or adds a badge on the tab; setting it to an empty string removes the badge text.

$stable.description: Kotlin Compose compiler-generated stability marker. Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

visible.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

selected_text_color.description: Sets the text color of this tab's label when the tab is in the selected state, passed to CycleSwitcherData.selectedTextColor. Accepted values: a color string such as a design-token path or hex value. Changing it alters the label color of the currently active tab.

background_color.description: Sets the background color of this individual tab in the CycleSwitcherView, passed to CycleSwitcherData.backgroundColor. Accepted values: a color string such as a design-token path or hex value. Changing it updates the fill color behind this tab.

deselected_text_color.description: Sets the text color of this tab's label when the tab is not selected, passed to CycleSwitcherData.deselectedTextColor. The final value is this field's value concatenated with the 'addition' field (if addition is null, the literal string 'null' is appended). Accepted values: a color string such as a design-token path or hex value. Changing it alters the label color of inactive tabs.

content_id.description: Links this tab to its offer screen content by referencing a content_id in 'optimus_screen_content' (or 'optimus_half_screen_content'). Also used as the CycleSwitcherData.id key and the tab-selection identifier when the user switches tabs. Accepted values: any string matching a content entry in the screen-content settings. Changing it swaps the subscription offer content displayed when this tab is selected.

show_unselected_state_badge.description: Controls whether the badge remains visible when this tab is in the deselected state, passed to CycleSwitcherData.showUnselectedStateBadge. Accepted values: true or false; defaults to false when null. Setting it to true keeps the badge visible even when the tab is not active.

title.description: Displayed as the label text on this tab in the CycleSwitcherView, passed to CycleSwitcherData.title. Accepted values: any string. Changing it updates the text the user sees on the tab (e.g. 'Yearly', 'Monthly').

gradient_type.description: Controls the gradient visual effect applied to this tab in the CycleSwitcherView, passed to CycleSwitcherData.gradientType. Accepted values: any string recognized by the CycleSwitcherView renderer. Changing it alters the gradient styling of the tab background.

selected.description: Determines which tab is initially selected and whose content is displayed when the screen first loads; the ViewModel finds the tab where selected is true and loads its content. Accepted values: true or false; exactly one tab in the array should be true. Changing which tab is true alters the default visible offer on screen open.

addition.description: Appended to the deselected_text_color value when constructing CycleSwitcherData. Accepted values: any string or null. When non-null, the string is concatenated onto the end of deselected_text_color; when null, the nullable concatenation produces a 'null' string suffix on deselected_text_color.

tabs_bg_color.description: Sets the background color behind the entire CycleSwitcherView tab bar, passed to CycleSwitcherData.tabsBgColor and stored as switcherBackgroundColor. Accepted values: a color string such as a design-token path or hex value. Changing it updates the background fill of the tab switcher row.

$stable.description: Kotlin Compose compiler-generated stability marker. Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

package_sku.description: The Google Play or web store product identifier used to initiate the purchase flow and fetch pricing details. Accepted values: any valid store product ID string (e.g. a Google Play subscription SKU). Changing this field changes which store product is purchased when the user taps the CTA button, and alters all dynamically resolved price strings displayed on the radio button and CTA.

button_texts.description: Ordered list of promotion configurations for this package; the app selects the first promotion the user is eligible for (checked via offer eligibility and free-trial status), falling back to the entry with promotion_type "default". Changing the list order or contents determines which promotional text, pricing, and CTA the user sees on the radio button and subscribe button.

promotion_type.description: Determines the promotion category, which controls eligibility logic and UI behavior such as toggle availability and discount display. Accepted values: "default", "free_trial", "introductory", "discount", "custom"; any other value is treated as "default". Changing this affects which users see this promotion: "free_trial" enables the free-trial toggle component if present, "discount" triggers lookup of a comparison package for crossed-out pricing, and "default" is used as the final fallback.

cta_text.description: Primary call-to-action button title text, which may contain special price placeholder characters that are dynamically replaced with actual pricing from the store. Accepted values: any string, optionally containing price-replacement tokens recognized by PaymentUseCase.replaceSpecialCharacter. Changing this updates the main text shown on the primary subscribe/purchase button at the bottom of the offer screen.

cta_sub_text.description: Subtitle text displayed below the primary CTA button title, supporting the same dynamic price-replacement tokens as cta_text. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the secondary line of text on the primary subscribe button.

secondary_cta_action.description: Determines the payment action type for the secondary CTA button. Accepted values: "web" (opens web-based payment flow) or "in_app_purchase" (opens native Google Play purchase flow); any other value defaults to "in_app_purchase". Changing this switches whether tapping the secondary CTA button initiates a web checkout or a native in-app purchase.

secondary_cta_subtitle.description: Subtitle text for the secondary CTA button, supporting dynamic price-replacement tokens. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the subtitle line on the secondary CTA button.

short_text.description: Compact text displayed on the right side of the radio button, combined with short_sub_text (separated by a newline) to form the right-side label. Supports dynamic price-replacement tokens and crossed-out price formatting. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the right-aligned text area on the radio button row.

secondary_cta_title.description: Title text for the secondary CTA button. When this field is non-empty and the native-to-web payment config is enabled, it activates the web payment sync flow for this promotion. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the main text on the secondary CTA button; setting it to empty disables the web payment sync flow.

description.description: Descriptive text shown as the CTA description area, with dynamic price-replacement token support. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the description text rendered near the CTA button section of the offer screen.

android_offer_id.description: Google Play offer identifier used to verify user eligibility for this promotion via SubscriptionRepo.isUserEligibleForOffer. Accepted values: any valid Google Play offer ID string, or "free_trial_offer" for free-trial web eligibility checks. Changing this affects which users qualify for this promotion; if the user is not eligible for the specified offer, the app skips to the next promotion in the list.

title.description: Main title text displayed on the radio button, supporting dynamic price-replacement tokens and crossed-out price formatting when a promotional_package_id is present. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the primary text label shown on the subscription radio button option.

promotional_package_id.description: References the id of another PackageApiModel entry used for discount comparison pricing. When promotion_type is "discount" and this field is non-empty, the app loads the referenced package and uses its pricing to show crossed-out original prices. Accepted values: an id string matching another PackageApiModel's id field, or empty string. Changing this alters which package's price is shown as the crossed-out comparison price on the radio button and CTA text.

$stable.description: Kotlin Compose compiler-generated stability marker. Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

subtitle.description: Subtitle text displayed on the radio button below the title, supporting dynamic price-replacement tokens and crossed-out price formatting. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the secondary text line on the subscription radio button option.

short_sub_text.description: Secondary compact text appended to short_text with a newline separator to form the right-side label on the radio button. Supports dynamic price-replacement tokens. Accepted values: any string, optionally with price-replacement tokens. Changing this updates the second line of the right-aligned text on the radio button row; if empty, only short_text is shown without a line break.

cta_action.description: Determines the payment action type for the primary CTA button. Accepted values: "web" (opens web-based payment flow, resolving the package ID to the web product) or "in_app_purchase" (opens native Google Play purchase flow); any other value defaults to "in_app_purchase". Changing this switches whether tapping the primary CTA button initiates a web checkout or a native in-app purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this package from radio button configurations (matched against the subscription button's package_info_id) and from promotional_package_id references in discount promotions. Accepted values: any unique string. Changing this breaks the link between the offer screen's radio button components and this package, causing the radio button to show no text or pricing data.

$stable.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect. This is an internal Kotlin Compose compiler stability marker automatically added to data classes.

ic_url.description: URL of the custom icon image to display on the close button, loaded via loadImage in OptimusScreenFragment.initCloseButton. Accepted values: any valid image URL string; if null or absent, loadImage is skipped and the button falls back to the default drawable (ic_close_white_bg) defined in the layout XML. Changing this replaces the close button icon on the subscription offer screen with the image at the provided URL.

show.description: Controls whether the close button is visible on the subscription offer screen, checked in OptimusScreenFragment.initCloseButton. Accepted values: true (button is set to VISIBLE, alignment/margins/icon are applied, and a click listener that calls closeOffer is attached) or false (the if-block is skipped entirely and the button remains in its XML-default GONE state). Setting this to false removes the close button, preventing the user from dismissing the offer screen via the close button.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this close button configuration from the remote settings list keyed by "subscription_screen_close_button" in OptimusScreenRepoImpl.getComponentById. Accepted values: any non-empty string matching a componentId referenced by the screen configuration's close_button.id field in OptimusScreenComponentsDataModel. Changing this value causes a different close button configuration to be selected (or no configuration if no matching id is found), which affects visibility, alignment, margins, and icon of the close button.

top_margin.description: Top margin in density-independent pixels (dp) applied to the close button view via setCloseButtonMargins, converted to physical pixels using the toPx extension. Accepted values: any integer representing dp; if null, the default XML layout margin of 8dp is preserved. Increasing this value pushes the close button further down from the top edge of the subscription offer screen.

alignment.description: Horizontal alignment of the close button on the subscription offer screen, deserialized via CloseButtonAlignmentModel and applied via setCloseButtonAlignment using ConstraintSet. Accepted values: "left" (maps to CloseButtonAlignment.LEFT — the default, keeps the button anchored to the start/left edge per the XML constraint) or "right" (maps to CloseButtonAlignment.RIGHT — clears the start constraint and anchors the button to the end/right edge of the parent). Changing from left to right moves the close button from the top-left corner to the top-right corner, and causes side_margin to be applied as marginEnd instead of marginStart.

side_margin.description: Horizontal margin in density-independent pixels (dp) applied to the close button via setCloseButtonMargins, converted to physical pixels using the toPx extension. When alignment is "right", this value is applied as marginEnd; otherwise ("left" or null) it is applied as marginStart. Accepted values: any integer representing dp; if null, the default XML margins of 8dp (both marginStart and marginEnd) are preserved. Changing this value adjusts the horizontal spacing between the close button and the nearest screen edge.

subtitle.description: Not consumed by titleComponentMapper when this object is loaded from the offer_screen_titles settings key — changing it has no visible effect for titles. Subtitles are loaded separately from the offer_screen_subtitles settings key using subtitleComponentMapper, which reads subtitle.text and subtitle.color from its own SubscriptionTitleApiModel instances. However, on the full Optimus screen (OptimusScreenFragment), if the title object is null, the subtitle is promoted to the title position (resolvedTitle = title ?: subtitle) and no subtitle is displayed. On the half screen this swap does not occur. Also not consumed when loaded from offer_screen_descriptions — the descriptionMapper only reads the title field.

color.description: Not consumed when loaded from the offer_screen_titles settings key — changing it has no visible effect. When loaded from offer_screen_subtitles and the subtitle is displayed (i.e., the parent title object is not null on full screen), color is applied normally.

new_field.description: New required field added to TextConfigApiModel. Deserialized from the "new_field" JSON key. Non-nullable with no default value — if absent in the JSON payload, Gson sets it to null at runtime which will cause a NullPointerException when accessed, since the Kotlin type is declared as non-nullable String.

text.description: Not consumed when loaded from the offer_screen_titles settings key — changing it has no visible effect. When loaded from offer_screen_subtitles and the subtitle is displayed (i.e., the parent title object is not null on full screen), text is displayed as the subtitle.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this title component from the array stored under offer_screen_titles. Matched against the component ID from the Optimus screen content configuration (optimus_screen_content → title → id) via getComponentById, which looks up the component directly from the offer_screen_titles settings key (the previous getComponentByIdForExperiment fallback that first tried offer_screen_titles_{componentId} has been removed). Also used when this model is loaded under the offer_screen_descriptions settings key for the description component. Accepted values: any non-empty string that corresponds to a title.id or description.id reference in the screen content config. If no item in the array matches the referenced component ID, the resulting TextConfig is null and the title view is hidden (GONE) on the offer screen.

title.description: Holds the text and color configuration for the main title displayed on the Optimus subscription offer screen. Mapped via titleComponentMapper into a TextConfig(text, color), reading only title.text and title.color (subtitle is ignored). Also read by descriptionMapper when this model is loaded from offer_screen_descriptions. If this object is null, the mapper produces TextConfig with empty strings via orEmpty(), and the title view remains visible but displays empty text. The title view is hidden (GONE) only when the entire SubscriptionTitleApiModel is not found by id or is disabled via is_enabled. On the full Optimus screen (OptimusScreenFragment), if title is null the subtitle value is used as the displayed title instead (resolvedTitle = title ?: subtitle), and the subtitle view is hidden. On the half screen (OptimusHalfScreenFragment), no such swap occurs. When shown, the title renders with Typography T6 and FontWeights SEMI_BOLD, centered horizontally with 24dp margins.

color.description: The color applied to the main title text, resolved via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy(path=color, defValue=PositiveColors.base.default). Accepted values: a CascadeColorPackage design-token path string; if null, empty, or unrecognized, the mapper converts null to empty string via orEmpty() and the color system falls back to CascadeColorPackage.PositiveColors.base.default. Changing this value alters the text color of the title TextView on the Optimus offer screen.

new_field.description: New required field added to TextConfigApiModel. Deserialized from the "new_field" JSON key. Non-nullable with no default value — if absent in the JSON payload, Gson sets it to null at runtime which will cause a NullPointerException when accessed, since the Kotlin type is declared as non-nullable String.

text.description: The display text for the main title on the Optimus offer screen. Accepted values: any string or null. A null value is converted to empty string via orEmpty() in titleComponentMapper, so the title view will still be visible but display no text — the view is hidden (GONE) only when the parent SubscriptionTitleApiModel is not found by id, not when this field is null. On the full Optimus screen, if the parent title object is null, the subtitle object's text is displayed as the title instead. Changing this value directly updates the title label the user sees, rendered with Typography T6 and SemiBold weight.

badge_url.description: URL of a badge image displayed below the banner placeholder text, centered horizontally over the banner area. Accepted values: any valid image URL string; null or absent hides the badge. Changing this replaces the 104×64dp badge icon shown beneath the placeholder text on the Optimus offer screen.

is_shadow_enabled.description: Controls whether a bottom-to-top gradient shadow overlay is drawn on top of the banner image. Accepted values: true or false (defaults to false when null). Setting to true makes a gradient view visible at the bottom of the banner, fading from the screen's positive base color to transparent, giving a fade-out effect over the banner image.

$stable.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

banner_placeholder.description: Contains the text and color configuration for the large overlay text displayed centered on top of the banner image area. Mapped via bannerPlaceHolderMapper into a TextConfig entity and rendered as a bold PicsartTextView. If null, the placeholder text view remains hidden.

$stable.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

color.description: Cascade Design System color path used to set the text color of the banner placeholder. Accepted values: a dot-notation color path string resolved by CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy(); falls back to PositiveColors.base.default if the path is empty or unrecognised. Changing this alters the color of the large bold text overlaid on the banner.

new_field.description: New field added to the text configuration object within the banner placeholder. Accepted values: any string. Deserialized from the "new_field" JSON key. Its effect on rendering is not yet consumed in current UI code beyond being part of the TextConfigApiModel.

text.description: The display string shown as a bold centered overlay on the banner area of the Optimus offer screen. Accepted values: any string; empty or null results in an empty text view. Rendered at Typography.T11 by default, but auto-upgrades to Typography.T10 if the text wraps to exactly 2 lines. Changing this updates the prominent heading text the user sees on top of the banner image.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this banner placeholder component from the remote settings list. Matched against the component_id specified in the Optimus screen configuration (optimus_screen_content). Accepted values: any non-empty string that corresponds to a bannerPlaceholder.id in the screen config. Changing this breaks the lookup if it no longer matches the screen config, causing the banner placeholder to not render.

schema.description: List of toggle configurations for the Optimus subscription offer screen, stored under remote settings key 'offer_promotion_toggle'. Each entry defines a free-trial or package-switching toggle that appears alongside radio buttons on the offer screen. The same model is used for both the main package toggle and the trial reminder toggle, selected by matching the 'id' field against different component IDs in the screen configuration.

disabled_state.description: Configuration applied when the toggle switch is in the OFF (unchecked) position. Contains the text content and visual styling for the toggle in its disabled state. The disabled_state.data.package_id is critically used to link this toggle to a specific radio button by matching against the radio button's packageInfoId.

view.description: Visual styling applied to the toggle component when in the disabled (unchecked) state or when the toggle is muted (a non-matching radio button is selected).

accent_color.description: Cascade Design System color path applied to the PicsartSwitch track tint when the toggle is in the unchecked state. Used in getToggleColorList() via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy(path=accentColor, defValue=BackgroundColors.tint3) to build a ColorStateList for the switch track. Accepted values: a valid Cascade DS color path string. Changing this value alters the switch track color when the toggle is unchecked OFF.

text_color.description: Cascade Design System color path applied to the toggle label text when the toggle is muted (i.e., a radio button other than the associated one is selected). Resolved via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy(path=textColor, defValue=PremiumColors.base.default). Accepted values: a valid Cascade DS color path string. Changing this value alters the text color of the toggle label when the toggle is inactive/muted.

data.description: Text content and package reference for the toggle's disabled (unchecked) state. The title is displayed as the toggle label when unchecked, and the package_id is used to determine which radio button this toggle is associated with.

subtitle.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect. The value is mapped to ToggleDataEntity.subtitle but is never read by any UI component.

description.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect. The value is mapped to ToggleDataEntity.description but is never read by any UI component.

package_id.description: Identifies the base subscription package that this toggle is associated with. OptimusScreenUseCaseImpl compares this value against each radio button's genericSubscriptionButton.packageInfoId to determine which radio button gets the free-trial toggle: 'buttonData.genericSubscriptionButton?.packageInfoId == screenData.toggle?.disabledState?.data?.packageId'. Accepted values: a valid package ID string matching a radio button's package_info_id from 'subscription_button'. Changing this value changes which radio button the toggle is linked to — the toggle will only appear active when the matching radio button is selected, and the free-trial data will be fetched for that button.

title.description: Text displayed on the freeTrialTextView PicsartTextView label next to the toggle switch when it is unchecked OFF. Set in observeForToggleStateChange via setFreeTrialText(disabledState.data.title). Accepted values: any string (displayed as-is). Changing this value updates the label text shown next to the toggle when it is in the OFF state.

enabled_state.description: Configuration applied when the toggle switch is in the ON (checked) position. Contains the text content and visual styling for the toggle in its enabled state. When the user checks the toggle, the title from this state replaces the toggle label text, the accent_color is applied to the switch track, and the package_id is used to fetch the free-trial subscription package for the continue button.

view.description: Visual styling applied to the toggle component when in the enabled (checked) state or when the toggle is not muted (the matching radio button is selected).

accent_color.description: Cascade Design System color path applied to the PicsartSwitch track tint when the toggle is in the checked state. Used in getToggleColorList() via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy(path=accentColor, defValue=PremiumColors.base.default) to build a ColorStateList for the switch track. Accepted values: a valid Cascade DS color path string. Changing this value alters the switch track color when the toggle is checked ON.

text_color.description: Cascade Design System color path applied to the toggle label text when the toggle is not muted (i.e., the associated radio button is currently selected). Resolved via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy(path=textColor, defValue=PremiumColors.base.default). Accepted values: a valid Cascade DS color path string (e.g., 'positive.base.default'). Changing this value alters the text color of the toggle label when the toggle is active/enabled.

data.description: Text content and package reference for the toggle's enabled (checked) state. The title is displayed as the toggle label, and the package_id identifies the free-trial package that replaces the regular package when the toggle is ON.

subtitle.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect. The value is mapped to ToggleDataEntity.subtitle but is never read by any UI component.

description.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect. The value is mapped to ToggleDataEntity.description but is never read by any UI component.

package_id.description: Identifies the free-trial subscription package activated when the toggle is ON. OptimusScreenUseCaseImpl calls subscriptionPackagesUseCase.execute(enabledState.data.packageId) to fetch the free-trial package details (price, title, promotion info) which are then displayed on the continue/CTA button. Accepted values: a valid package ID string matching an entry in 'subscription_packages_list'. Changing this value switches which free-trial package is offered when the toggle is checked — the continue button text, price, and purchase flow will reflect the new package.

title.description: Text displayed on the freeTrialTextView PicsartTextView label next to the toggle switch when it is checked ON. Set in observeForToggleStateChange via setFreeTrialText(enabledState.data.title). Accepted values: any string (displayed as-is). Changing this value updates the label text shown next to the toggle when it is in the ON state.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this toggle configuration from the list. OptimusScreenRepoImpl calls getComponentById matching this value against the screen configuration's 'switch_package_toggle.id' or 'trial_reminder_toggle.id' field. Accepted values: any non-empty string that matches a corresponding component ID in 'optimus_screen_content'. Changing this value will break the lookup, causing the toggle to not appear on the offer screen.

enabled.description: Determines the initial checked state of the PicsartSwitch toggle when the screen loads. The code checks 'toggle?.enabled == true' in SubscriptionRadioButtonViewModel.setToggleData(); when true AND a matching free-trial package exists, the switch starts checked, radio buttons are visually muted, the continue button shows free-trial package data, and the trial reminder toggle becomes enabled. Accepted values: true or false (defaults to false if null). Setting to true pre-selects the free-trial package on screen load; setting to false shows the regular package selected.

schema.description: A JSON array of secondary button configurations for the Optimus subscription offer screen. Each entry is fetched from remote settings under the key 'offer_screen_secondary_button' and matched by id to the screen's component layout. The matched entry drives the underlined text link shown below the subtitle, controlling its label, color, and tap behavior (deep-link navigation or opening the tiers half-screen).
schema.title: SubscriptionSecondaryButtonApiModel

items.description: A single secondary button configuration object that defines the appearance and behavior of the underlined text button displayed below the subtitle on the Optimus offer screen. It is resolved by id from the remote settings list and mapped through secondaryButtonMapper into a SecondaryButtonEntity consumed by OptimusScreenFragment.initSecondaryButton.

$stable.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

color.description: Defines the text color of the underlined secondary button. The value is passed to CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy(path=..., defValue=BackgroundColors.tint3), which resolves it as a design-system semantic color token path. Accepted values: any valid CascadeColorPackage path string (e.g. a semantic token path recognized by the design system); if empty or unrecognized, it falls back to CascadeColorPackage.BackgroundColors.tint3. Changing this value alters the color of the underlined secondary button text on the offer screen.

action.description: A deep-link or hook URL that determines the navigation when the user taps the secondary button. In initSecondaryButton the code checks if this value is null or empty: if non-empty, HookUtils.openHook(context, action) is called to navigate to the specified deep-link; if null or empty, the button instead opens the subscription tiers half-screen dialog using the touchpoint field. Accepted values: any valid hook/deep-link URL string, or null/empty to trigger the tiers half-screen flow. Changing this value redirects the secondary button tap to a different deep-link destination, or switches between hook navigation and opening the tiers half-screen.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this secondary button configuration from the remote settings list keyed by 'offer_screen_secondary_button'. OptimusScreenRepoImpl.getComponentById matches this value against the componentId referenced in the screen layout (OptimusScreenComponentsDataModel.secondaryButton.id). Accepted values: any non-empty string that corresponds to an id value in a screen's secondary_button component reference; if no entry matches, the secondary button is not rendered (visibility GONE). Changing this value links or unlinks the button configuration to a particular Optimus screen layout, making the secondary button appear or disappear.

text.description: The display label shown as an underlined text link below the subtitle on the Optimus offer screen. The mapper passes this value (or empty string if null) to SecondaryButtonEntity.text, and configureSecondaryButton wraps it in a SpannableString with UnderlineSpan applied across its full length, rendered in a centered TextView. Accepted values: any string; null is coerced to empty string via orEmpty(). Changing this value updates the visible underlined text of the secondary button; setting it to empty produces a clickable but blank underlined area.

touchpoint.description: An analytics and navigation identifier used only when the 'action' field is null or empty. When the secondary button is tapped and no action hook is set, this value is passed into SubscriptionAnalyticsParam.touchPoint and used to open the subscription tiers half-screen dialog (SubscriptionOfferHalfScreenTiersFragment via SubscriptionHalfScreenManager.getSubscriptionTiersHalfScreen). Accepted values: any string; null is coerced to empty string via orEmpty(). Changing this value alters which tiers half-screen variant is opened and changes the touchpoint value reported in the subscription_secondary_click analytics event.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this feature-highlights component from the remote-settings list keyed by "offer_screen_feature_highlights". Accepted values: any non-empty string that matches a component reference in the OptimusScreenComponentsDataModel.featureHighlights.id field. Changing it breaks the lookup, so the feature-highlights section will not appear on the offer screen.

items.description: Ordered list of feature highlight entries rendered vertically inside FeatureHighlightsView. Each entry becomes a row with an icon, title, and subtitle. Accepted values: array of feature-highlight item objects (may be null or empty). If null or empty, the entire feature-highlights view is hidden (visibility GONE).

icon_url.description: URL of the icon image displayed at the start of the feature-highlight row (24×24 dp ImageView). Accepted values: any valid image URL string; if null or empty, the ImageView remains but loads no image. Changing it swaps the icon shown next to the feature title and subtitle.

subtitle.description: Text configuration for the secondary description line displayed below the title in each feature-highlight row, rendered with Typography.T9 and FontWeights.REGULAR. If the text is empty, the subtitle view is hidden (visibility GONE). Changing this object alters the subtitle copy and color of the feature-highlight row.

color.description: Color path resolved via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy() to set the subtitle text color. Accepted values: a Cascade design-token path string (e.g. "positive.base.default"); falls back to PositiveColors.base.default if empty or invalid. Changing it updates the subtitle text color in the feature-highlight row.

new_field.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

text.description: The subtitle string displayed beneath the title in a feature-highlight row. Accepted values: any string; if empty, the subtitle PicsartTextView is hidden. Changing it updates the secondary descriptive text shown for this feature highlight.

title.description: Text configuration for the primary label displayed next to the icon in each feature-highlight row, rendered with Typography.T8 and FontWeights.SEMI_BOLD. If the text is empty, the title view is hidden (visibility GONE). Changing this object alters the title copy and color of the feature-highlight row.

color.description: Color path resolved via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy() to set the title text color. Accepted values: a Cascade design-token path string (e.g. "positive.base.default"); falls back to PositiveColors.base.default if empty or invalid. Changing it updates the title text color in the feature-highlight row.

new_field.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

text.description: The primary label string displayed next to the icon in a feature-highlight row. Accepted values: any string; if empty, the title PicsartTextView is hidden. Changing it updates the main feature name or heading shown for this highlight entry.

action.description: A hook or deep-link URI that is opened via HookUtils.openHookDirect when the user taps the footer text. Accepted values: any valid hook/deep-link string recognized by HookUtils, or null/empty to disable tap behavior. Changing this value alters what screen or action is triggered on footer tap; setting it to empty or null removes the click listener so the footer becomes non-interactive.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this footer component from the remote settings list keyed by "offer_screen_footers". Accepted values: any non-empty string that matches the component ID referenced in the screen configuration's footer.id field. Changing this value breaks the association between the screen configuration and this footer entry, causing the footer to not be found and therefore hidden.

text.description: Container object holding the display text and its color for the footer label shown below the radio buttons on the Optimus offer screen. Accepted values: a JSON object with "text", "color", and "new_field" sub-fields, or null to hide the footer. Changing this object modifies the visible footer content and styling; setting it to null hides the footer entirely.

color.description: A CascadeColorPackage path string used to resolve the footer text color via CascadeColorPackage.getColorOrPackageBy; falls back to BackgroundColors.tint3 if the path is empty or invalid. Accepted values: any valid CascadeColorPackage color path (e.g., "positive/base/default") or an empty string for the default tint3 color. Changing this value updates the footer text color on the offer screen.

new_field.description: Not consumed in current source code — changing it has no visible effect.

text.description: The literal string displayed in the footer TextView on the Optimus offer screen; when empty the entire footer view is hidden (visibility GONE). Accepted values: any non-empty string for visible footer text, or an empty string to hide the footer. Changing this value updates the footer label the user sees, or hides the footer if set to empty.

width.description: Specifies the desired display width of the logo image in density-independent pixels (dp), which is then converted to physical pixels via toPx. Accepted values: any positive integer representing dp units. If changed, the logo ImageView on the Optimus offer screen will render at the new width; both width and height must be non-null for the custom size to take effect, otherwise the ImageView uses its default wrap_content dimensions.

id.description: Unique identifier used to look up this logo entry from the remote settings list keyed by 'offer_screen_logos'. Accepted values: any non-empty string that matches a component reference id in OptimusScreenComponentsDataModel.logo.id. If changed, the repo will resolve a different LogoApiModel entry (or none if no match is found), which changes or hides the logo displayed on the Optimus offer screen.

url.description: The image URL loaded into the logo ImageView via the loadImage call on the Optimus offer screen. Accepted values: any valid image URL string (the image loader handles network URLs). If changed to a different URL the logo image updates accordingly; if set to an empty string or null the logo ImageView is hidden (visibility GONE).

height.description: Specifies the desired display height of the logo image in density-independent pixels (dp), which is then converted to physical pixels via toPx. Accepted values: any positive integer representing dp units. If changed, the logo ImageView on the Optimus offer screen will render at the new height; both width and height must be non-null for the custom size to take effect, otherwise the ImageView uses its default wrap_content dimensions.

all_files.role: UI entry point for the Optimus subscription offer screen, displays banners, radio buttons, toggles, and handles purchase flow.
Manages screen state by fetching optimus screen data and emitting it to the fragment via StateFlows.
Interface defining the contract for fetching and processing optimus screen data with tabs support.
Implements OptimusScreenUseCase by composing repo data with package details and payment text processing.
Interface for fetching raw optimus screen configuration from remote settings.
Fetches optimus screen config from RemoteSettings, deserializes all component API models by settings key, and assembles the OptimusEntity.
Domain entity representing a single optimus offer screen with all its visual components and RadioButtonsType sealed interface.
Domain entity holding tab-based optimus screen data including single and multi-tab offer screens.
API model deserialized from 'optimus_screen_data' remote settings key, defines tab-based screen structure. Contains OptimusSingleTabsModel.
API model deserialized from 'optimus_screen_content' remote settings key, defines component references for a single screen. Contains ComponentApiModel.
Base interface for all component API models, providing a common id field for component lookup.
Extension function putIntoBundle that maps WebPaymentInfo into a Bundle for subscription purchase navigation.
API model deserialized from 'offer_screen_banners' remote settings key, defines banner image/action data. Contains BannerValuesModel.
Maps SubscriptionBannerApiModel to SubscriptionBanner domain entity. Contains bannerComponentMapper and bannerValuesMapper.
API model deserialized from 'offer_screen_titles' remote settings key, defines title and subtitle text config.
Maps SubscriptionTitleApiModel to TextConfig domain entity. Contains titleComponentMapper and subtitleComponentMapper.
API model deserialized from 'offer_banner_placeholder_text' remote settings key, defines banner placeholder text and badge.
Maps SubscriptionBannerPlaceholderApiModel to BannerPlaceHolderEntity domain model.
API model deserialized from 'offer_promotion_toggle' remote settings key, defines toggle enabled/disabled states. Contains ToggleStateDataModel, ToggleData, ToggleView.
Maps SubscriptionToggleApiModel to ToggleEntity. Contains toggleComponentMapper, stateMapper, toggleDataMapper, toggleViewMapper.
API model deserialized from 'subscription_screen_close_button' remote settings key, defines close button visibility and position.
Maps CloseButtonModel to SubscriptionCloseButton domain entity.
API model deserialized from 'offer_screen_secondary_button' remote settings key, defines secondary action button.
Maps SubscriptionSecondaryButtonApiModel to SecondaryButtonEntity domain entity.
API model deserialized from 'subscription_button' remote settings key, defines radio button CTA styling. Contains ButtonViewApiModel, CtaButtonViewApiModel, ButtonStyle.
Maps SubscriptionButtonApiModel to RadioButtonEntity. Contains radioButtonsMapper and subscriptionButtonMapper.
Domain entity for banner placeholder display data including text config and shadow settings.
Domain entity for secondary button display data including text, color, action, and touchpoint.
Domain entity for toggle component with enabled/disabled states. Contains ToggleStateEntity, ToggleDataEntity, ToggleViewEntity.
Domain entity for radio button component combining button styling with resolved package/pricing data.
Domain model for subscription button combining CTA buttons, button view styling, and package info reference. Contains CtaButton, ButtonView.
Sealed interface defining radio button indicator alignment options: Left, Right, None.
API model for radio button style config with button list, purchase-on-select flag, indicator alignment. Contains RadioButtonItemApi.
Processes subscription packages by resolving prices, promotions, and web payment info for radio button display text.
Domain model holding resolved button text with pricing, promotion type, and CTA models. Contains CtaButtonModel, WebPaymentInfo.
Interface for syncing native packages with web payment config. Contains SyncFlowResult data class.
Implements ProcessWebPaymentSyncUseCase, resolves web product IDs and promo eligibility using NativeToWebConfigRepo.
Fetches raw package list from 'subscription_packages_list' remote settings and maps to domain models.
API model deserialized from 'subscription_packages_list' remote settings key, defines package SKU and promotion texts. Contains PromotionApiModel.
Domain models for package data. Contains PackageInfo, Promotion, CtaConfig, CtaAction enum, PromotionType sealed class.
Maps PackageApiModel to PackageInfo domain model. Contains packagesDataMapper and promotionMapper.
Fetches native-to-web payment configuration from remote settings for web payment sync flow.
Custom UI component that renders radio buttons, toggles, trial reminder toggle, and CTA subscription button.
ViewModel managing radio button selection state and toggle interactions within OptimusButtonsComponent.
Custom view that displays legal terms and conditions text with styled links at the bottom of the offer screen.
Handles all subscription analytics tracking including offer open, button click, and secondary button events.
Implements SubscriptionOpenWrapper by launching the shop subscribe activity via SubscriptionClassProvider.
Shared API model for text configuration with text content and color properties, used by title, subtitle, and banner placeholder API models.
Shared API model for badge configuration used by RadioButtonItemApi for badge text, color, variant, and visibility.
Enum model for banner types (image, video, etc.) with asBannerType() mapping to domain BannerType.
Enum model for close button alignment with asCloseButtonAlignment() mapping to domain CloseButtonAlignment.
Interface for launching the subscription purchase activity from offer screens.
Interface for creating subscription half-screen dialog fragments for tier-based offers.
Interface for common offer activity operations like closing the offer and controlling loading state.
Serializable params passed to the fragment containing analytics params, content variant, and feature configuration.
Domain entity for subscription banner with banner type and list of banner values (url, action, placeholder). Contains BannerValues.
Implements SubscriptionHalfScreenManager by instantiating the appropriate half-screen fragments.
Contains TextConfig data class and BannerType enum used across the subscription feature for text styling and banner type identification.
Domain entity for close button display config (show, alignment, icon, margins). Contains CloseButtonAlignment enum.
Domain entity for tab switcher data including id, title, badge, selection state, and gradient/color config.
Domain model for processed text with spannable content used for crossed-out prices and styled subscription text.
Configuration entity for text size parameters used by SubscriptionTextProcUseCase when creating price spans.
Domain entity for radio button badge with gradient type, variant, visibility, text config, and deselected state colors.
Interface for payment text processing, replaces special characters in text with actual subscription prices.
Interface for creating styled subscription text spans with crossed-out prices and formatted pricing.
Koin DI module binding OptimusScreenRepo, OptimusScreenUseCase, OptimusScreenViewModel, and SubscriptionRadioButtonViewModel.
Koin DI module binding PackagesRepo, NativeToWebConfigRepo, ProcessWebPaymentSyncUseCase, and GetProcessedPackagesUseCase.
XML layout defining the Optimus screen UI structure with banner, title, subtitle, radio buttons container, secondary button, close button, tab switcher, and legal text views.

tree.role: UI entry point for the Optimus subscription offer screen, displays banners, radio buttons, toggles, and handles purchase flow.

children.role: Manages screen state by fetching optimus screen data and emitting it to the fragment via StateFlows.
Interface defining the contract for fetching and processing optimus screen data with tabs support.
Implements OptimusScreenUseCase by composing repo data with package details and payment text processing.
Interface for fetching raw optimus screen configuration from remote settings.
Fetches optimus screen config from RemoteSettings, deserializes all component API models by settings key, and assembles the OptimusEntity.
API model deserialized from 'optimus_screen_data' remote settings key, defines tab-based screen structure. Contains OptimusSingleTabsModel.
API model deserialized from 'optimus_screen_content' remote settings key, defines component references for a single screen. Contains ComponentApiModel.
API model for radio button style config with button list, purchase-on-select flag, indicator alignment. Contains RadioButtonItemApi.
Shared API model for badge configuration used by RadioButtonItemApi for badge text, color, variant, and visibility.
Base interface for all component API models, providing a common id field for component lookup.
API model deserialized from 'offer_screen_banners' remote settings key, defines banner image/action data. Contains BannerValuesModel.
Enum model for banner types with asBannerType() mapping to domain BannerType.
Maps SubscriptionBannerApiModel to SubscriptionBanner domain entity. Contains bannerComponentMapper and bannerValuesMapper.
Domain entity for subscription banner with banner type and list of banner values. Contains BannerValues.
API model deserialized from 'offer_screen_titles' remote settings key, defines title and subtitle text config.
Shared API model for text configuration with text content and color properties.
Maps SubscriptionTitleApiModel to TextConfig. Contains titleComponentMapper and subtitleComponentMapper.
API model deserialized from 'offer_banner_placeholder_text' remote settings key, defines banner placeholder text and badge.
Maps SubscriptionBannerPlaceholderApiModel to BannerPlaceHolderEntity domain model.
Domain entity for banner placeholder display data including text config and shadow settings.
API model deserialized from 'offer_promotion_toggle' remote settings key. Contains ToggleStateDataModel, ToggleData, ToggleView.

Maps SubscriptionSecondaryButtonApiModel to SecondaryButtonEntity.

Maps LogoApiModel to LogoEntity domain model.
Domain entity for logo display data with URL, width, and height.
Maps SubscriptionTitleApiModel to TextConfig for description component, reusing the title API model structure.
Processes subscription packages by resolving prices, promotions, and web payment info for radio button display text.
Fetches raw package list from 'subscription_packages_list' remote settings and maps to domain models.

Domain models: PackageInfo, Promotion, CtaConfig, CtaAction enum, PromotionType sealed class.
Interface for syncing native packages with web payment config. Contains SyncFlowResult.

Fetches native-to-web payment configuration from remote settings.

Interface for creating styled subscription text spans with crossed-out prices.

Configuration entity for text size parameters used when creating price spans.
Interface for payment text processing, replaces special characters in text with actual subscription prices.

Domain entity for tab switcher data including id, title, badge, selection state, and gradient/color config.

Contains TextConfig data class and BannerType enum used across the subscription feature.
Handles all subscription analytics tracking including offer open, button click, and secondary button events.
Interface for launching the subscription purchase activity from offer screens.

Implements SubscriptionHalfScreenManager by instantiating the appropriate half-screen fragments.

ViewModel managing radio button selection state and toggle interactions within OptimusButtonsComponent.
Custom view that displays legal terms and conditions text with styled links at the bottom of the offer screen.
Interface for common offer activity operations like closing the offer and controlling loading state.
Serializable params passed to the fragment containing analytics params, content variant, and feature configuration.
Extension function putIntoBundle that maps WebPaymentInfo into a Bundle for subscription purchase navigation.
